chat.onADateBody: Your date with {name} is still going — your progress is safe.
chat.reopenFailed: We couldn't reopen the conversation just now. Try again to pick it back up.
chat.planSub: Pick someone, choose where to meet, and begin.
chat.walletNote: Wallet: {wallet} · a nicer venue costs money but can impress the right person.
chat.availBanner: {name} {reason} — pick someone else or come back tomorrow.
chat.outOfEnergy: You're out of energy for today — End the day from the menu to rest, then come back tomorrow.
chat.evalDetail: Mood: {mood}. {summary} ({count, plural, one {# memory saved} other {# memories saved}})
chat.endingNote: You've built something that lasts. Read it any time in the phone's Endings — and the game goes on.
chat.breakupNote: Give them space — keep reaching out and you may win them back.
chat.youEndedBody: It's over for now. They'll need some space — but if you keep reaching out, you may be able to win them back later.
chat.walkoutNote: The date is over — this reflected badly on you.
chat.leftEarlyBody: The date wasn't landing and they slipped away early — it fizzled out, and that set things back a little.
chat.leftEarlyNote: Try reading them better next time.
chat.dtrBackfireTitle: It didn't go as you hoped.
chat.dtrNotYet: Not yet — give it a little more time.
chat.giftEmpty: No gifts on hand — buy something at the Shop first.
chat.newDateTitle: Start a fresh date with someone else
chat.sayHello: Say hello to start the conversation…
chat.rocksNote: The relationship feels strained — turn it around soon.
chat.breakupConfirmBody: You're about to end things. {name} just responded above — confirm to break up for real, or keep going.
chat.intentComing: How you want to come across

share.addsSummary: Adds {summary} to your almanac. Your existing worlds and people are untouched.
share.importCharsToggle: {count, plural, one {Import the # character too} other {Import the # characters too}} — {worlds, plural, one {uncheck to take just the world & its locations} other {uncheck to take just the worlds & its locations}}
share.exportSub: Saves a {ext} file you can hand to anyone — it carries the content, never your save.
share.exportCharsToggle: {count, plural, one {Include each world's characters (uncheck to share just the world & its locations)} other {Include each world's characters (uncheck to share just the worlds & its locations)}}
share.title: Title
share.noteOptional: (optional — a message for whoever opens it)
share.notePlaceholder: e.g. The full Lumen Quarter cast — let me know what you think!

profile.memorialBanner: {name} is gone. They're remembered here — your history together remains, but you can't reach them anymore.
profile.needsSpace: {name} needs some space — you can't go on a date yet, but you can still text them.
profile.openToReconcile: {name} is open to seeing you again — keep reaching out (texts + a date) and you may rekindle things.
profile.onTheRocksBanner: Things are on the rocks with {name}. Turn it around soon — a few good dates — or the relationship could end.
profile.noPersonality: No personality or traits have been filled in for {name}.
profile.noProfileBody: Flavor like appearance, texting style, and quirks hasn't been added — generate it in the editor.
profile.noHistoryBody: Go on a date with {name} to start writing your story together.
profile.noMemoriesBody: Go on a date and end it to capture memories, or add some in the editor.
profile.memoriesHint: What {name} remembers about you — written manually or captured automatically after a date.
profile.memoriesCollapsed: {count, plural, one {# memory kept — tap <0>Show</0> to read them.} other {# memories kept — tap <0>Show</0> to read them.}}
profile.confirmDeleteBody: This removes their memories and your relationship too. This can't be undone.
profile.confirmDuplicateBody: This creates a saved copy of {name} now and opens it. The copy is kept whether or not you edit it further.

characters.title: People
characters.blurb: The hearts you keep close — every face you can call on for a date.
characters.emptyTitle: No one in your life yet
characters.emptyCreatorBody: Bring someone into being to start dating.
characters.emptyPlayerBody: Switch to Creator mode (Phone → Settings) to add characters.
characters.soulCount: {count, plural, one {<0>#</0> soul in your almanac} other {<0>#</0> souls in your almanac}}
characters.unassignedBody: These belong to no world, so they don't show in any roster.
characters.placeInWorld: Place one into {world} to start dating them.
characters.enterWorld: Enter a world to place them.
characters.confirmDeleteBody: This removes their memories and your relationship too. This can't be undone.
characters.confirmDuplicateBody: This creates a saved copy of {name} now and opens it in the editor. The copy is kept whether or not you save your edits.
characters.confirmDiscardBody: This permanently removes the unsaved draft. Anything already saved is untouched.

minigames.title: Minigames
minigames.blurb: Play together to grow closer — and earn a little money.
minigames.errOnDate: You're on a date with {name} — finish it on the Date tab first.
minigames.outOfEnergy: You're out of energy for today — end the day to play again tomorrow.
minigames.onDateBanner: You're on a date with {name} — finish it on the Date tab before playing.
minigames.outOfEnergyTitle: Out of energy — end the day to begin a new one.
minigames.noRewards: Nothing earned this time — but you played beautifully.

shop.title: Shop
shop.blurb: Trinkets, gifts, and small luxuries to bring along on your dates.
shop.saved: {count, plural, one {Saved # item to the shop.} other {Saved # items to the shop.}}
shop.genHintPre: Creates a batch of lore-friendly items based on
shop.genHintPost: . Nothing is saved until you press Save — review and tweak first.
shop.themeHint: Optional — e.g. “cozy autumn café trinkets” or “relics from the old harbor”.
shop.themePlaceholder: Describe the kind of items you want…
shop.reviewHint: {count, plural, one {Review the # generated item. Uncheck any you don't want, edit the rest, then Save.} other {Review the # generated items. Uncheck any you don't want, edit the rest, then Save.}} Effects are balanced behind the scenes and shown read-only.
shop.name: Name
shop.emptyCreator: Generate a batch of items above to stock the shelf.
shop.emptyPlayer: Check back later — new wares arrive in time.
shop.inStock: {count, plural, one {# item in stock} other {# items in stock}}
shop.confirmDeleteBody: This removes the item from the shop.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.blurb: Your keepsakes. Give a gift in person on a date, or send one in a text.
inventory.emptyBody: Buy something from the shop first.
inventory.giveHint: Give on a date or by text

dashboard.eyebrow: A lamplit almanac of the heart
dashboard.fallbackLine: Pick someone, and see where the evening goes.

line.morning: A fresh day is open. Who will you spend it with?
line.afternoon: The afternoon is yours — make a little time for someone.
line.evening: The lamps are lit. A fine hour for a date.
line.night: The night is quiet. Send a text, or rest until tomorrow.

dashboard.createSomeone: Create someone to begin your story.
dashboard.switchToCreator: Switch to Creator mode in the phone's Settings to add people.
dashboard.morePeople: There {count, plural, one {is # more person} other {are # more people}} available — head to the <0>Date tab</0> to see the full list.

worldSelector.kicker: A lamplit almanac of the heart
worldSelector.title: Choose a world
worldSelector.sub: Each world is its own story — its own people, its own calendar, its own you. Step into one to begin, and come back here any time to switch.
worldSelector.settingsSub: Set your language, accent, and the model endpoint Heartmorrow talks to. These apply to every world.
worldSelector.startNewSub: Set up a fresh story and persona
worldSelector.emptyNote: Your almanac is empty. Start your first world above to begin.
worldSelector.deleteBody: This permanently removes the world and your progress in it — relationships, money, messages, and history. This cannot be undone.
worldSelector.deleteCharsLabel: Also delete this world's characters. Leave unchecked to keep them — they'll move to Unassigned (People → Unassigned) so you can place them in another world.

worldOnboarding.nameRequired: Give your world a name to continue.
worldOnboarding.chooseSource: Choose a world to start from.
worldOnboarding.modeCloneSub: Copy an existing world & its cast
worldOnboarding.cloneFlavor: Pick a world to copy. Its setting, lore, and the people in it are duplicated into a brand-new save — your money, relationships, and history start fresh.
worldOnboarding.nameNewSavePlaceholder: e.g. The Lumen Quarter — take two
worldOnboarding.blankFlavor: A world is the stage your story plays out on — a town, a season, a mood. You can flesh out its lore, locations, and people later; for now, just give it a name and a feeling.
worldOnboarding.summaryHint: Optional — what kind of place is this?
worldOnboarding.summaryPlaceholder: A cozy arts district where neighbors become something more.
worldOnboarding.toneHint: Optional — the emotional key of the story.
worldOnboarding.conjureFlavor: Give a spark — a vibe, a place, a premise — and generate a whole setting, lore, and locations to start from. No people are created; you'll bring those in later. Edit anything before you continue.
worldOnboarding.ideaHint: Optional — the more you give, the more it has to work with.
worldOnboarding.ideaPlaceholder: e.g. A storm-battered lighthouse town where everyone keeps a secret — autumn, slow-burn, a little melancholy.
worldOnboarding.rulesHint: How this world works — may be blank for an ordinary setting.
worldOnboarding.globalNotesHint: The always-in-mind briefing the narrator keeps for every scene.
worldOnboarding.noLocations: No locations — regenerate to get some.
worldOnboarding.noNotes: No notes — regenerate to get some.
worldOnboarding.personaFlavor: This is a fresh start — a separate you, with your own money, keepsakes, and history in this world. Tell us who you are here.
worldOnboarding.sexualityHint: Decides which characters a romance can deepen with. Leave unspecified to date freely.
worldOnboarding.aboutYouHint: Optional — a sentence or two the people you date will sense about you.
worldOnboarding.aboutYouPlaceholder: A sound engineer who just moved to town. A good listener; bad at sitting still.
worldOnboarding.importFlavor: Know someone from another world you'd like to meet again? Copy them in as a fresh face — a new beginning, no history carried over. Skip this and your world stays as it is.
worldOnboarding.noImport: You don't have anyone to import yet. Skip ahead — you can always create people once you're in.
worldOnboarding.welcomeFlavor: The lamps are lit, {persona}. {summary} Here's the shape of a day before you step in:
worldOnboarding.newChapter: A new chapter is yours to write.
worldOnboarding.blankPageCreator: This world is a blank page — no one lives here yet. Once you step inside, head to People to create the characters who call it home.
worldOnboarding.blankPagePlayer: This world is a blank page — no one lives here yet. Turn on Creator mode in the phone's Settings to populate it with people to meet.

howToPlay.date: Spend your days meeting people — dates and shared activities each cost a little energy.
howToPlay.people: Talk, and they remember. Relationships warm or cool over time, and drift if you neglect them.
howToPlay.phone: Your phone holds texts, mail, and a living social feed that keeps moving as the days pass.
howToPlay.shop: Buy gifts and keepsakes from the shop — your money and bag are yours alone in this world.
howToPlay.recap: When your energy is spent, end the day to rest, advance time, and see what happened around town.
howToPlay.worlds: Switch worlds any time from the selector — each one is a completely separate story and save.

bench.repetitionTitle: Similarity to this character's previous line
bench.usingDefault: Using the built-in default — runs score against this unless you save your own.
bench.estFoot: * Some token counts are chars/4 estimates (your endpoint didn't report usage).
bench.noRuns: No saved runs yet. Run a bench and save it to compare later.
bench.deleteRunBody: This permanently removes the saved bench run.
bench.title: Heartmorrow Benchmark
bench.intro: Run the real prompts this game asks of your model - the rapport judges, the date evaluator, the texting replies, the world & creator generators - against a fixed sample cast in Lanternford. The point of this is to measure whether or not your model is, on a technical level, able to produce the structured output needed, and on a subjective level, produce interesting and fun prose which is not repetitive and does not go off the rails. Some models can easily produce the structured output but also produce very dry or bad prose.
bench.llmPlayerTitle: Let a second model play the player side of dialogue (dynamic but less comparable).
bench.runSaved: Run saved — find it under History.

debug.title: Debug
debug.intro: Inspect assembled prompts, recent game events, and import/export your local data.
debug.estimatorIntro: Builds the real prompt for each common interaction and counts its tokens, so you can see how close each one runs to your model's context window. With measure live on, counts are your model's exact usage.prompt_tokens; otherwise they're a rough chars/4 estimate.
debug.charDataSourceHint: Whose real world/relationship/history is used to assemble the prompts.
debug.contextWindowHint: Your model's loaded context length. Rows whose prompt + reply exceed it are flagged.
debug.measureLive: Measure live (exact tokens — needs the endpoint up)
debug.totalNote: Total = prompt tokens + the reply budget (your Max tokens setting). If Total exceeds the context window, the model will truncate the oldest context (or fail) during that interaction.
debug.importReplaces: Import replaces all local game data with the file's contents.
debug.importComplete: Import complete. Reload the app to see changes everywhere.

worldEditor.title: World editor
worldEditor.intro: Define the setting. World/lore notes are sent to the model as reference data, not as instructions.
worldEditor.pickOrCreate: Pick a world from the list, or create one.
worldEditor.name: Name
worldEditor.featuresNote: Optional life-and-money systems. Turn these off for a world where they wouldn't fit — the apps disappear and authored property/companies simply lie dormant.
worldEditor.featureProperty: Property — rent or buy places, collect rent, date there for a buff
worldEditor.featureStock: Stock market — invest in fictional in-world companies
worldEditor.featureCasino: Casino — wager money on slots, blackjack, roulette & video poker
worldEditor.dailyCapHint: Total staked per day · up to {max}
worldEditor.genNote: Describe what you want. The model uses this world's tone, lore, and existing locations to invent new ones — they appear below for you to review and edit before saving.
worldEditor.promptPlaceholder: e.g. cozy rainy-evening spots near the old harbor district
worldEditor.generatedAdded: {count, plural, one {Added # generated location below — review, then Save world to keep them.} other {Added # generated locations below — review, then Save world to keep them.}}
worldEditor.deleteBody: The world, its notes, and your progress in it are removed. This cannot be undone.
worldEditor.deleteCharsLabel: Also delete this world's characters. Leave unchecked to keep them — they'll move to Unassigned (People → Unassigned) so you can place them in another world.
worldEditor.locDescPlaceholder: Describe the vibe, sights, and feel of this place.
worldEditor.photoHint: Upload a photo of this place — it shows in the date location picker and the scene.
worldEditor.notesCallout: Notes are reference data the model can read — not instructions. Higher importance surfaces sooner.

characterEditor.intro: All notes here are treated as character data in prompts - never as instructions to the model. You should fill this out with a lot of detail so the LLM can breathe as much life into your characters as possible. Data from this profile is surfaced in quite a few areas, and it will only be as good as what you put in. Putting slop in will give slop out. The only thing you should be cognizant of is that the more data you put here, the longer the prompts will be. You can press preview prompt in the top right to see how long a full prompt would be.
characterEditor.name: Name
characterEditor.sexualityHint: Gates who romance can deepen with. Players discover it through play.
characterEditor.worldHint: A character with no world is unassigned and won't appear in any world until you place them (recover them under People → Unassigned).
characterEditor.relStyleHint: Monogamous characters may get jealous if they learn you're dating others.
characterEditor.portraitGenNote: Use Generate to draft a whole character — from this portrait, some pasted or uploaded text, or both. Fills every tab for you to review before saving.
characterEditor.genIntro: Draft a complete character from a portrait, reference text, or both — then review and edit every tab before saving. Provide at least one source.
characterEditor.genPortraitHint: A vision model describes the image and the look grounds the character's appearance. Set a vision model in Settings (it falls back to your main model).
characterEditor.genTextHint: Paste or upload anything to base the character on — a wiki article, a character sheet, or a few notes. Files are read as plain text only.
characterEditor.genTextPlaceholder: Paste a description, article, or character sheet here…
characterEditor.genOverwriteWarn: Generating will replace the name, identity, personality, traits, profile, and dating stats in your current draft. Your portrait, expressions, connections, and employment are kept — and nothing is saved until you press Save.
characterEditor.genFileError: Couldn't read that file as text. Try a different file.
characterEditor.expressionsNote: These are the fixed expressions the date evaluator can pick. Assign an image to any you want shown; leave the rest blank to use the default portrait.
characterEditor.creatorNotesHint: Whatever extra guidance you want to give to the model; never shown verbatim to players.
characterEditor.guardednessHint: How slow they are to warm up on a date. Higher = they open up, trust, and flirt only once it's earned, and a date with them starts cooler. Doesn't change how fast a bad date sours.
characterEditor.weatherNote: Sets their mood on those days and nudges outdoor dates. ♥ = loves it, ✕ = can't stand it.
characterEditor.profileNote: Flavor that makes {name} feel alive — how they look, text, and show up online. Used by the in-world phone feed.
characterEditor.textingStyleHint: How they write texts & posts — distinct from how they speak aloud.
characterEditor.onlinePersonaHint: How they behave on a social feed.
characterEditor.chemistryNote: Tasteful physical/sensory notes plus the little human details.
characterEditor.baseStatsNote: These six traits define the character's dating persona. They shape how much relationship progress Together activities yield (each activity favors certain stats), pick the character's favorite minigame (their highest stat), and color how they talk on dates. They may also drive additional systems in the future.
characterEditor.connectionsNote: How {name} relates to others in the world — drives gossip and edge-aware jealousy.
characterEditor.exCanonBody: Let other characters establish facts about {name} by revealing them as their ex on a date (e.g. a date mentions their ex used to smoke → it becomes true here, and they react if you bring it up). Off by default — their truth stays immutable. Reversible anytime.
characterEditor.relStatsLater: Relationship stats appear after the character is created.
characterEditor.memoriesSaveFirst: Save the character first to add manual memories.
characterEditor.memoryPlaceholder: e.g. The player loves rainy days too.
characterEditor.employmentNote: What {name} does for work. Coworkers (anyone sharing the same workplace) tend to run into each other — this feeds the world simulation. Leave unemployed if they don't work.
characterEditor.workplaceHint: Characters who share a workplace are coworkers.
characterEditor.characterGenerated: Character generated — review every tab and Save.

head.title: Settings
head.blurb: Configure various settings used by the game, including locale, appearance, and LLM endpoint and model settings.

toast.noModels: Connected, but the endpoint returned no models.
toast.loadedModels: {count, plural, one {Loaded # model — pick one in the Model field.} other {Loaded # models — pick one in the Model field.}}

appearance.head: Appearance
appearance.lede: How Heartmorrow looks and reads — these apply across the whole app, not just your phone.
appearance.languageHint: The language of the interface. Conversations and stories still play out in English, unless the character's profile specifically instructs them to speak another language.
appearance.accentHint: Tints highlights, buttons, and romance cues throughout the app.

mode.head: Mode
mode.playDesc: hides creation/editing tools (no deleting characters mid-game).
mode.creatorDesc: shows them. Also in Phone → Settings.
mode.creator: Creator mode

nsfw.head: Adult content (NSFW)
nsfw.lede: When enabled, the model may generate mature/explicit content during dates — but only once your relationship with a character is advanced enough. Propositioning a stranger or acquaintance will still make them walk out.
nsfw.modelHint: Best paired with an abliterated / “uncensored” model. A censored or safety-tuned model may still refuse explicit content even with this toggle on.

tragic.head: Tragic outcomes (self-harm)
tragic.lede: When enabled, sustained, severe mistreatment of someone who loved you (repeated heartbreak, cheating, cruelty) can spiral — with many clear warnings and chances to stop — into a character taking their own life, permanently memorializing them. The act is never depicted. Leaving them be or treating them kindly always pulls them back. Off by default.

persona.head: Your persona
persona.lede: How characters see you — your name, pronouns, and notes are shared with everyone you meet.
persona.name: Your name
persona.sexualityHint: Decides which characters a romance can deepen with.
persona.notesHint: Optional — anything you want characters to know about you.

bench.head: Heartmorrow Benchmark
bench.blurb: Benchmark how your model handles the real prompts this game runs — the rapport judges, date evaluator, and generators — against a fixed sample. Score the judges against your own baseline, watch a date play out, and track tokens, latency, and tokens/sec. Save runs to compare models.

chat_completions.name: OpenAI-compatible
chat_completions.desc: OpenAI /chat/completions — LM Studio (/v1), Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, or any compatible server.

lmstudio.name: LM Studio
lmstudio.desc: LM Studio's native API — richer model listing (loaded state, context, quant) and per-response speed stats. Point Base URL at …/api/v0.

anthropic.name: Anthropic
anthropic.desc: Anthropic Messages API — api.anthropic.com or any compatible proxy/gateway. Needs an API key + anthropic-version.

responses.name: Responses
responses.desc: Reserved for the OpenAI Responses API — not implemented yet; falls back to chat/completions.

roles.usingMain: This role uses the Main configuration. Turn it on to give it its own endpoint and model.

evaluator.blurb: The relationship judges — how a date scored, whether a text landed, and gift / breakup / farewell reactions. Route them to their own model or endpoint (say, a small fast local model) while prose stays on your main model.

vision.blurb: Image-based generation, like drafting a character from a portrait or reacting to an image text. Enable to use a vision-capable model on a wholly separate endpoint; otherwise the base vision model on the Main tab is used.

fields.apiKeyHintSet: A key is set. Leave blank to keep it.
fields.anthropicVersionHint: Sent as the anthropic-version header. Leave at the default unless your endpoint needs a newer revision.
fields.modelHint: Type any model name, or pick from the loaded list below.
fields.pickFrom: {count, plural, one {Pick from # model…} other {Pick from # models…}}
fields.visionModelHint: Optional - used for image-based generation (e.g. drafting a character from a portrait or for a character to respond to an image text). Leave blank to reuse the model above.
fields.advancedSamplingHint: Optional. Leave a field blank to let the endpoint use its own default. top_k / min_p / repeat penalty are honored by llama.cpp, LM Studio, Ollama, and vLLM but ignored or rejected by the official OpenAI API.
fields.structuredModeHint: json_object works with most local servers.
fields.dropSchemaHint: Perf test for json_schema mode only: the grammar already enforces the shape, so the duplicated schema text in the prompt is redundant. Skipping it shrinks the prompt (faster prefill). No effect in json_object / prompt_only.
fields.dropSchemaLabel: Skip the duplicate schema text (json_schema mode)
fields.retryLimitHint: Retries after a malformed/invalid structured response.
fields.rapportHint: How often a date reads how your last message landed (updates the vibe + their expression). 'Every message' is most responsive; 'periodic' keeps replies snappier with one fewer model call per turn.

nsfwModal.title: Enable adult (NSFW) content
nsfwModal.intro: This is a private, local, single-user game. Content is generated by your own local model and never leaves your machine. To continue, please confirm both of the following:
nsfwModal.ackContent: I understand that with adult content enabled, the local model may generate explicit, sexual, or otherwise inappropriate material, and that DSim does not filter or guarantee its output.
nsfwModal.ackAge: I affirm that I am of legal age to view adult content in my jurisdiction.
nsfwModal.footnote: Best paired with an abliterated / “uncensored” model — a censored model may still refuse even with this on. Adult content is only ever generated once your relationship with a character is advanced enough; propositioning a stranger or acquaintance will still make them walk out.

tragicModal.title: Enable tragic outcomes
tragicModal.intro: This adds a heavy, optional consequence: if you repeatedly and severely mistreat a character who became deeply attached to you — and ignore the escalating warnings, including a worried friend reaching out — they may take their own life and be permanently memorialized. The act itself is never shown. Being kind, giving them space, or simply stopping always pulls them back from it.
tragicModal.ack: I understand this content deals with suicide as a consequence of in-game abuse, and I want it enabled. I can turn it off at any time.
